summary: #2a person-bootstrap path A end-to-end run on 119 real client ID photos (Asian faces, Chinese filenames). Validates the cold-start gallery seed at production scale and supplies the first real-world detection-rate baseline (an Asian-face counterpart to the #2 / #2c synthetic-fixture baselines, partially de-risking #2c-asia).

inputs.source: client employee uniform ID photos, Asian, supplied as id_photos.tar.zip

#2_face_distribution_baseline.context: 42-photo wo_raw run on the LFW-funneled + jack139 fixture; mixed Western (32) + Eastern (10) buckets

#2c_eastern_bucket.context: 10 jack139/face-dataset Asian-celebrity crops at the default fixture resolution (256x256 bicubic upscaled); bottlenecked at SCRFD detection — blocks #2c-asia

#2a_real_id_photos.context: 119 real client ID photos (controlled lighting, frontal pose, ~94 % portrait crops, JPEG/PNG natively); SCRFD has no detection failures on this domain

secondary_findings.summary: persons.photo_count is not incremented by the owner-known person bootstrap path.
secondary_findings.observed: After 119 successful uploads with owner_type=person + owner_id, SUM(persons.photo_count) = 0 (expected 119); each photos row is in status='matched' and identity_embeddings count is correct (119/119), so the recognition path itself is healthy.
secondary_findings.hypothesis: The persons.photo_count denormalised counter is likely only updated on a different path (e.g. an unmatched -> matched reconciliation in the wo_raw flow) and not on the owner-known bootstrap path that #2a takes. Existing client UIs that surface 'photos per person' would under-count for cold-start uploads.
secondary_findings.impact: Cosmetic / UI-only at this point; does not affect recognition correctness, gallery seeding, or wo_raw recall.
secondary_findings.action: Filed for King to triage; no fix in this commit (out of scope for the path-A evidence run).

validation_queries: SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM photos WHERE project_id=$PID GROUP BY status
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM identity_embeddings WHERE source='initial' AND owner_type='person' AND source_project=$PID
SELECT n_emb, COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT owner_id, COUNT(*) AS n_emb FROM identity_embeddings WHERE source='initial' AND owner_type='person' AND source_project=$PID GROUP BY owner_id) s GROUP BY n_emb
SELECT target_type, COUNT(*) FROM detections WHERE project_id=$PID GROUP BY target_type
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM persons p WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM identity_embeddings ie WHERE ie.owner_id=p.id AND ie.source='initial' AND ie.owner_type='person' AND ie.source_project=$PID)

prereqs: 119 ID photos under /tmp/id_photos (filenames matching ^(?P<eid>[0-9]{8})[+-]?(?P<name>[\u4e00-\u9fff]+)(?P<dept>[\u4e00-\u9fff]*)\.(?:jpg|jpeg|png)$, case-insensitive ext)
f1photo serve booted on 127.0.0.1:18799 with bundled-pg, F1P_BUNDLED_PG_PASSWORD set, admin user 'smoke_admin'/'smoke-admin-pwd-12345' bootstrapped
empty project created (e.g. code='id-photo-2a'); PROJECT_ID stored in $PID
reproducer.command: python3 tools/ingest_id_photos.py --mode ingest --photos-dir /tmp/id_photos --base-url http://127.0.0.1:18799 --admin-user smoke_admin --admin-pwd smoke-admin-pwd-12345 --project-id $PID --manifest-out /tmp/id_manifest.json --report-out /tmp/id_ingest_report.json --continue-on-error
reproducer.poll_drain: SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM photos WHERE project_id='$PID' GROUP BY status -- expect matched=119 within ~30s
